behavior_summary: Provides runtime behavior for a transient light object: a long-running per-light process (LIGHT::0080) that increments an internal tick counter each suspend/tick, periodically causes a camera 'quake' effect, occasionally triggers a small randomized sub-effect, and terminates after a fixed lifetime.

Contains an event handler (LIGHT::gotHit) that guards against repeated hits, validates overlap with an attacker, branches on the light item's animation/frame to pick one of multiple responses (spawn the light process + play SFX, or trigger a LightningBolt effect + play different SFX), optionally queries an NPC's facing direction to parametrize a sub-call, and may destroy the item depending on the hit arguments.
A global guard flag alreadyHitLightBeam prevents re-entrant or duplicate hit handling while the gotHit handler runs.

Spawn and cleanup are coordinated: LIGHT::gotHit spawns the LIGHT::0080 process (the per-light runtime) and also spawns a FREE::1C19 helper with the returned pid (inferred to schedule freeing/cleanup).
notable_control_flow: U8P_LIGHT::LIGHT::0080 runs as a suspension-looping process: increment tick_counter each suspend, check for lifetime expiry (tick == 100) to call Camera::stopQuake() and exit.
Every 10 ticks LIGHT::0080 computes quake_index = tick / 10 (with a wrap adjustment when > 5) and calls Camera::startQuake(quake_index) to start a camera quake effect.

Each LIGHT::0080 loop performs an independent chance check (urandom(50) == 37) that, if true, computes small random args (urandom(4), urandom(8) variants) and calls METHOD::0080 with those args and the item/process reference for an occasional visual/behavioral sub-effect.

U8P_LIGHT::LIGHT::gotHit early-returns if alreadyHitLightBeam is set or if Item::overlaps(attacker) fails; otherwise it sets alreadyHitLightBeam=1, branches on Item::getFrame() to select behavior, spawns processes and schedules cleanup, then clears the guard before returning.
open_questions: Exact semantics and side-effects of METHOD::0080 (called from both the random sub-effect in LIGHT::0080 and from gotHit) — appears to spawn/configure a visual or behavior sub-effect but details are not explicit.
Precise behavior of spawned FREE::1C19 helper (assumed to free/schedule cleanup of a pid) and whether it applies a timeout or immediate cleanup.
Meaning and game-impact of the camera quake intensity mapping (why quake_index wraps when >5) and whether other systems observe the quake state.

Exact interpretation of gotHit's second parameter (BP+0Ah) beyond the observed branches: value==1 triggers Npc::getDir+METHOD::0080, value>0 triggers Item::destroy; other values' intended use are inferred but not canonical.

main_function.name: U8P_LIGHT::0080
main_function.purpose: Run-time controller for an individual light instance: maintain lifetime (tick-based), start periodic camera quakes with computed intensity, occasionally trigger randomized visual/behavioral sub-effects, and stop the quake and exit when lifetime ends.
entry_conditions: Spawned by U8P_LIGHT::LIGHT::gotHit (spawn call observed) when the light should enter its active runtime state.
Registers process-item association and excludes the process from some default processing at start (engine 'set info / process exclude' calls).

function_map.name: U8P_LIGHT::0080
function_map.role: Per-light runtime process that manages lifetime, camera quake effects, and occasional randomized sub-effects.

function_map.name: U8P_LIGHT::gotHit
function_map.role: Event handler for hit interactions on the light item: guards re-entrancy, validates overlap, selects response by item frame, plays SFX, spawns LIGHT::0080 or triggers LightningBolt, optionally calls METHOD::0080, and schedules cleanup.

function_map.name: Camera::startQuake
function_map.role: Engine call to start a camera quake with a signed intensity argument (used by LIGHT::0080 periodically).

function_map.name: Camera::stopQuake
function_map.role: Engine call to stop any active camera quake (called by LIGHT::0080 when lifetime ends).

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Random number generator used to introduce chance into occasional sub-effects inside LIGHT::0080 and to derive random args for METHOD::0080.

function_map.name: METHOD::0080
function_map.role: An object/method call invoked to spawn or configure a visual/behavioral sub-effect for the item; purpose inferred from usage in both runtime and gotHit paths.

function_map.name: U8P_LIGHT::playSFX
function_map.role: Play sound effects for different hit/frame outcomes in gotHit.

function_map.name: Item::overlaps
function_map.role: Check overlap between the item and an attacker/source; gating condition for gotHit handling.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Query the item's current animation/frame index used to select branching behavior in gotHit.

function_map.name: spawn
function_map.role: Engine spawn used to start scripts/processes; used to spawn LIGHT::0080 and FREE::1C19 (cleanup helper) from gotHit.

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawned helper that receives a pid (inferred to schedule freeing/cleanup of that pid); exact behavior inferred.

function_map.name: U8P_LIGHT::process LightningBolt
function_map.role: Spawned by gotHit for a specific frame range to perform a lightning effect/damage routine instead of spawning LIGHT::0080.

function_map.name: Npc::getDir
function_map.role: Query NPC facing/direction used by gotHit to compute an argument to METHOD::0080 when second param equals 1.

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Destroy the item instance; called by gotHit when the second parameter indicates immediate destruction.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the light's runtime process when a hit should activate the light behavior; returned pid is captured and passed to cleanup helper.

external_calls.purpose: Start a camera quake effect periodically (every 10 ticks) with intensity derived from tick/10 (with wrap adjustment).
Stop the camera quake when the light's lifetime expires (tick == 100) before the process exits.
Perform a chance check each loop (urandom(50) == 37) and generate random args (urandom(4), urandom(8)) for occasional sub-effects.
Invoke a visual/behavioral sub-effect with computed/random small arguments and the item/process reference when the random check succeeds.
Verify the attacker/source overlaps the item as a prerequisite for processing the hit.
Read the item's animation/frame index to select the hit response branch (spawn LIGHT::0080 vs. spawn LightningBolt vs. other behavior).
Play context-appropriate sound effects for different frame-based hit outcomes.
Spawn LIGHT::0080 (the runtime) and spawn FREE::1C19 with the returned pid for cleanup scheduling; spawn LightningBolt in certain frame cases.
Pass the spawned runtime pid to a cleanup helper (inferred to schedule freeing); used regardless of main branch to ensure proper cleanup.
When the second hit parameter equals 1, query NPC facing to compute a directional argument passed to METHOD::0080.
Destroy the item instance when the second hit parameter indicates immediate destruction (value > 0 and not the special-case 1).

reads.name: alreadyHitLightBeam
reads.likely_effect: Guard read to determine if a prior hit is being processed; prevents duplicate handling.

writes.name: alreadyHitLightBeam
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 at the start of gotHit to prevent re-entrancy and cleared to 0 at the end of the handler; coordinates single-handling semantics for hit events.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides runtime behavior for a transient light object: a long-running per-light process (LIGHT::0080) that increments an internal tick counter each suspend/tick, periodically causes a camera 'quake' effect, occasionally triggers a small randomized sub-effect, and terminates after a fixed lifetime.
